rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate AADL scope: component type for interface assumptions/guarantees, implementation for binding assignments.
Use only identifiers and types present in the AADL model; do not introduce package-qualified or undefined references.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture structure during AGREE annex fusion by aligning annex content with component type and implementation scopes.
Avoid introducing new data types, properties, or identifiers not defined in the input AADL model.
Do not assume or invent numeric constraints unless explicitly stated in the requirement and supported by the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing ports must be in component type scope; assignments must be in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not encode AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex structure (covered by KSYN-1)
AADL AS5506C standard references (covered by KSYN-7)
Defensive rules for Boolean comparisons or event ports (covered by KDEF-1, KDEF-5)
